reviews.review_msg: Bob is a funny guy, most people compare him to Eeyore based on his demeanour. I took this class in the summer so it was a bit odd, but overall he is a really nice prof and tries to make his lectures interesting. if you can take a class with him i highly recommend it hes very knowledgeable and has been around a while, he knows what hes talking about

Professor Muir was very kind and easy to reach out to. He responds to emails quickly and gives helpful feedback, if you ask for it. Read the rubric carefully for each assignment, pay attention to his slides/videos (majority of exam material is from there, not the textbook). It's definitely a GPA booster class, I enjoyed it and recommend it!

Bob Muir is an excellent professor! I have taken numerous ARCH courses taught by him from the 100 level to the 400 level. He is one of the best professors in the SFU Archaeology department, and his lectures are really interesting to sit through. 100% recommended!

TA's mark the assignments very harshly. They will deduct significant marks if you have improper referencing. This prof on the other hand, his always grumpy for some reason and deliberately wants to catch his students cheating. He would generate AI made papers for himself and would then cross check your assignment to see if they match.

I have taken many classes with him over my degree.
His lectures are where I have learned practical information that is applicable to a career, compared to some courses that seem like u are being set up to stay in academia.
Assignments are fairly graded and helped me learn the course content. Exams always reflect what we learned in class.

Bob is a great lecturer. Many struggle to see the charm and passion through the monotone voice but he genuinely cares about everyone doing well in his class. He does often look down when lecturing and does warn his students about this which I think is perfectly fine as he is more focused on conveying the material in a clear and understandable way.

Bob can be VERY hard to pay attention to because he's very monotone and doesn't look up for questions but don't be afraid to just speak out. I have always found him very lenient and helpful. He can actually be a lot of fun when he is comfortable so don't be turned off from his seemingly grouchy mood.

Lectures are very low-energy, if you raise your hand with a question he'll never see it, you have to verbally interrupt because he doesn't look up when he talks. It's not very engaging. If you make the effort to get to know him I think he is better, but that seems to be hard to do. He is definitely very knowledgeable about PNW archaeology.
Course assignments and readings are very practical and relevant. Challenging assignments.

took arch101 intersession & found it pretty easy, asynch w weekly discussions worth 35% & 3 fairly short assignments worth a total of 65%. bob is easy to reach out to, replies to emails quickly, & provides good feedback. weekly films were assigned which I enjoyed bc they were really interesting! def recommend this class for b-soc, good gpa booster

ARCH 331-PrehistoryBC All Rob wants to know is that you learned something from his lecture. Make sure you spend some time memorizing the map he covers in the beginning of the lecture for the map quiz. Midterm and final are easy. Just write everything you learned from the lecture and try to make them relevant to the questions, and you will do great!
I really enjoyed his lectures. Great mix of documentaries, and very clear articulation. Interesting.
Bob is a great lecturer, very responsive to questions, and shows his interest in the topics

This class is a 5 credit course, and you can definitely tell. He doesn't give you a lot of information for the quizzes and they are really tough. Assignments are okay but also graded quite hard. A very dry course. He is not the friendliest and not very approachable.

Interesting topic. Not an Arch major, just an elective. Watched documentaries once a week which were actually helpful. The book was somewhat helpful. Attend lectures, although they may be boring at times. Bob is open to questions about the exams and what not. TA took it a little too seriously though when marking. Good class to learn a bit!

Bob isn't the most interesting lecturer, but then he isn't boring either. Definitely average. He didn't provide a lot of criteria for the assignments and therefore I found myself sending him a lot of emails with questions. He was pretty easy when it came to marking for participation and helped prepare you for the midterm. Overall not a bad guy

Muir was okay, but his choice of TA was terrible. Very rude and not willing to help and made that clear. I worked so hard in this class and think the prof should go over some of the marking before he uses her again.
His TA marks hard on the essays, more emphasis on grammatical errors >.> He has the boring voice ever...

If you don't even have a remote interest in archaeologist, do yourself a favor and stay away from this course. Muir generaly has the "I hate teaching" and the TA marks way too hard. Doesn't help the fact there's no tutorials in this course for assignment help.

He seems like a nice guy, but he's a boring prof. He has a monotone voice, often stares at the ground while lecturing, and generally seems bored to be here. I had taken ARCH 100 with a different prof and it developed my interest in archaeology-but that was soon gone after taking this class. I wouldn't recommend taking a class with him at all.
Really nice professor, fare exam, must attend class.

He seems to find teaching a chore. He sighs loudly as he presents each slide. He also leaves each lecture early when he plays a movie. (It's embarassing.) Furthermore, does anyone else find it audacious and even immoral that he is asking a class of 30-odd students to write a paper that affirms his relevancy as an archaeologist?

Bob is an amazing prof! I loved the set up of his lectures and the labs were pretty much crystal clear, you can tell her has been teaching a long time but thats what makes his classes so structured and organized.
Very clear and helpful. I found his notes useful when studying for tests. Also, he doesn't pick on you if you're quiet and don't like speaking up in class.
Great teacher! Nice, helpful, and easy-going. He's one of my favourite arch teachers at SFU
I highly recommend Rob. If you are at school to learn and you want credit for all the effort you put in, you should definitly take classes with Rob.

easy to get an A, but you need to go to lectures and memorize lots of places and dates. quizes are sometimes difficult, but the final was much better. no assignment in this class which makes a great elective

Anyone who says anything bad about bob isn't in his class to learn. The amount of work bob does a semester could not be done by any other prof except for him. Yes.. he looks like he hasn't slept in 10 days all the time, but just shut up and listen to him and you'll learn he has a lot of important information you can learn.

Some of the material in ARCH 100 are interesting, but some are dull.  The quizzes are not that easy.  To do well you have to understand his lecture notes well. There are lots of places and names that you need to know.  If you are good at memorizing places, names, and some dates, then this course is good to take.

Easy prof. If you are lazy and don't want to study, this is a great course, provided you can stay awake in lecture. If you listen, you will be ready for his tests without any studying. Course is very easy, everything is linked together. Easy to score an A in the course. Material a bit dry, but overall not bad

Easy course, but very boring lectures. Great prof if u could put up with how boring he is and get notes taken down. To get high grades it would be best to attend all lectures and not just sleep through them. Textbook isnt used too much.

It's true that this course is about memorizing lecture notes. But u hv to memorize tons and tons of dates and cultures and names of some mysterious images etc. And his lectures are really boring with his monotone. It's pulling down my GPA crazily.

Don't listen to any of the negative comments about this guy. Bob is the best prof in this university. He understands students and make his course very easy. Just study lecture notes and you will be gareenteed an A or A+. They truly don't come any better than Bob at the university level. Its very hard to find good profs. Exams are cake.Itook 100+201

Really nice guy, had the most helpful slides ever! don't really need to read the book but if you study the slides (and pics!) you should be okay. No final or midterm just 4 25% quizzes (only one of which was difficult). Definatly recomend this class!

This prof is somewhat bored at teaching the material. He drones on and on but the notes are interesting. However DON'T USE HIS NOTES ON MIDTERMS OR FINALS. Most of the stuff u need 2 kno are gonna be in the textbook. So there4, dun even bother goin 2 lecture.

This course is a good elective to take esepcially if you take it with Bob.  Although readings are assigned, they are only informative and they almost never show up on the quizes.  You have 5 quizes all are lab based with the exception of the first one.  Labs are a time consuming but easy to get a good mark on if you put in the time.
waste of space...should be teaching...too bitter...acts like he hates his job and archaeology
a good gpa booster. Highly recommended. Also - the prof is a nice funny guy.
I love how this class was set up - 4 quizzes, 25% each. Bob is a great teacher... funny too. Quiz material was based all on lectures; the textbook was completely pointless.
Great professor. Any person who can make the evolution of corn seem anything less than mind-numbingly painful deserves a round of applause.

Bob is the best prof ever! Easy, concise and striaghtforward. He is very clear when he speaks and gets the point across well, lectures are worth going to. His mark breakdown is extremely easy, no midterms,finals,term papers,labs or tutorials. Just 4 quizzes worth 25% each which are only on 3 chapters at a time, which makes it easy. You'll thank me!

Quizzes are all from his power point slides (printing them off before class saves writing too many notes). He'll tell you to read the textbook, but it's not really necessary unless you want a great deal of background information. Do remember to study the pictures in his notes... he's not lying when he says he'll be putting slides on the quizzes!
I wish I were patient enough to take other classes with Bob.  Great sense of humour, and pretty easy marker.  He was really easy to talk to outside of class.

Bob is not only really nice to look at, he is a really great instuctor. He is clear and concise in what he is trying to get across to the class.  When I could take my eyes off him I found that what he had to say was very informed and highly interesting. I wish I could take a major in Bobology.
Notes are never released to students until 10 minutes before the class, which is useless. This course is easy only when u are excellent in pure memorization.
this man does not help...had his favorites that enjoy following him around kissing up to him...he seems to like which is pathetic...poor rep. of what archaeologists really are....
No Comments
this dudes a ****! he is very prompt on webct/emails. you learn something and get a good mark in the process.

Class could get boring at times, but he usually kept it going at a good pace. Really good sense of humour. Only real beef is with the instructions he gave for the second quiz: he told us not to worry about studying specific sites, then gave us a bunch of questions about specific sites! That wasn't cool. Overall a really good prof though.
THIS CLASS IS TOO BORING BUT ITS REALLY EASY YOU DONT EVEN HAVE TO GO TO CLASS TO GET AN A

I found the clas really great! Hes so clear on what he want you to know and what you need to know for the quizes. the content was really interesting too. Good pic for lecture. He made it really easy to do well in his course and he seems nice, has a good sense of unexpected humour too.knows what hes talking about for sure.Over all really great clas
An easy course. Study the notes and go to the lectures and you'll be fine. Bob's a great prof.

Went nuts on the second test- spite isn't nice Bob! However, an amazing lecturer, so clear and interesting. Can be funny at times, but also makes this "I want to destroy the world" face at the bigging of most classes. Is he really angry? We will never know. Also very helpful on webct and seems to know an aweful lot. He doesn't look like a potato!
Bob Rocks! Anyone who thinks he doesn't probably doesn't do well anyways. Bob makes it easy to learn and he'll join you at the pub for a post-lecture chat. And his dogs are cool.

I don't know why people dislike him so much.  He's super nice, relatively easy marker and tries to make the best of the material he is teaching. 372 is lets face it, not the most exciting material to learn about.  I would take another class with him!

Wow, what a tool!  this man was so freaking boring, if you have to take a class with him, (and I mean HAVE to) have at least 3 coffees before class or you won't make it!  Oh and he looks like a potato.
Well, first of all, he teaches arch, not anth. Second, he is a great prof, and if you're bored with him, he's probably bored with you, too.
I contemplated all the different ways I could hang myself from the rafters every time I had to sit through this man's class.

He is by far the most BORING prof I have ever had.  His lectures are equal to watching paint dry.  He told our class that he knows our writing so if we write any bad comments on his evaluation he'll know who it was....Is that fair or allowed?  Overall, taking a class with bob muir is like having a pigeon teach the class!  NO Thanks!

Interesting class. Easy midterm. Lecture material well chosen and available on WebCT. Great films shown in class, too. Bob always looks at the ground when speaking, but what can you expect--he's an archaeologist! Pretty good overall.
garbage
Not the easiest class but a very interesting one. Just make sure you go to lecture and you'll be fine.

Fair dividing up of grades between exams/assignments. Super nice guy who is normal--rare ontop of this mountain. Easygoing and approachable, made a crusty, dry, excrutiating subject fascinating and pleasurable. Very available outside of class time, always answers his e-mail.
Explains things in a way that make it easier to understand. Interesting and relevant stories that aid in understanding. Very approachable.
A good guy. His lectures are a bit boring, but he puts everything online and makes everything really clear which is helpful. Lots of movies, too, so a good oppertunity for sleep.
Very nice and understanding. Makes theory more bearable than it really is :)
super-nice guy, makes theory interesting.
On eof the best profs in the arcaheology department
Tries really hard to make a boring subject interesting
No Comments
Boring at first, but he kinda grows on ya. Good Power Point lectures. Divides marks up well - not just a midterm and a final.
